content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

September 13, 1988

Honorable Ralph O. Hill  
Mayor  
68 Washington Street  
P.O. Box 1293  
San Juan Bautista, CA 95045-1293

Re: Our File No. I-88-354

Dear Mayor Hill:

You have asked whether a reserve police officer who is a candidate for city council would be required to resign from the police force if elected to the city council.

The Commission provides written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question, or to his or authorized representative. (Section 83114(b).) Your inquiry does not appear to be authorized by the candidate in question. Therefore, we must decline to provide specific advice in response to your question. (Regulation 18329, copy enclosed.)

For your information, we have enclosed an advice letter and a pamphlet concerning the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths  
General Counsel

By: Kathryn E. Donovan  
Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:KED:plh

Enclosures

68 Washington St  
Box 1293  
San Juan Bautista, CA  
95045-1293  
408-623-4863  
8/26/88

Dear Ms. Kathryn E. Donovan,  
Council: Legal Division,

In our November City Election, one of the candidates is employed (part-time) as a Reserve Police Officer (a paid employee). If this person is elected to the City Council in November 1988 would he have to resign from the police force?

Sincerely yours,  
Ralph D. Hill  
Mayor San Juan Bautista
